title: Smoky Silky Thai Padron Peppers Curry
description: A silky, smoky onepot curry that brings the fresh bite of Padron peppers into a creamy Thai-inspired sauce.

ingredients.name: Padron peppers
ingredients.notes: trim stems and cut in half

instructions: Set your electric pressure cooker to sauté mode and heat 1 tbsp of olive oil.
Add diced onion, minced garlic, and ginger; cook until fragrant, about 2 minutes.
Add the halved Padron peppers and sliced red bell pepper; sauté until they soften, about 3 minutes.
Stir in red curry paste, soy sauce, and brown sugar; cook for 1 minute to meld flavors.
Pour in coconut milk and vegetable broth, stirring to combine.
Seal the lid and cook on high pressure for 3 minutes.
Quick‑release the pressure and open the cooker carefully.
Stir in lime juice and taste; adjust with more soy sauce or sugar if needed.
Transfer the curry to a serving bowl, drizzle toasted sesame oil over the top, and sprinkle chopped cilantro.
Serve hot over jasmine rice or noodles for a complete meal.
